title: Tangy Smoky Middle Eastern Lamb Pasta
description: A fragrant fettuccine tossed with smoky ground lamb and tangy harissa, finished with fresh herbs and lemon for a high‑protein, date‑night delight.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook fettuccine according to package instructions until al dente; drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium‑high heat. Add diced onion and bell pepper, sautéing until softened, about 4 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the ground lamb to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon. Cook until browned and cooked through, about 6 minutes. Drain excess fat if needed.
Stir in tomato sauce, harissa paste, smoked paprika, cumin, and coriander. Reduce heat to medium and simmer for 5 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
Return the cooked fettuccine to the skillet, tossing to coat the pasta evenly with the lamb sauce.
Season with sea salt, black pepper, lemon juice, and half of the chopped mint and parsley. Toss well.
Serve hot, garnished with the remaining mint and parsley, and a drizzle of extra olive oil if desired.
